Mora"}]}}, "gondolas": {"description": "Local transport within Vivec City between cantons", "stops": ["Arena", "Foreign Quarter", "Hlaalu", "Telvanni", "Temple"]}}
Balmora: {"description": "District seat of House Hlaalu and the second largest settlement on Vvardenfell after Vivec. The Odai River divides it into four areas: High Town, Commercial District, Labor Town, and nearby Moonmoth Legion Fort.", "services": ["Fighters Guild", "Mages Guild", "Thieves Guild (South Wall Cornerclub)", "Morag Tong", "Tribunal Temple", "Alchemist (Nalcarya of White Haven)", "Clothier (Milie Hastien)", "Blacksmith (Thorek)", "Armorer (Meldor)", "Bookseller (Dorisa Darvel)", "Pawnbroker", "Traders"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Ald'ruhn", "Silt Strider to Suran", "Silt Strider to Vivec", "Silt Strider to Seyda Neen", "Guild Guide to Ald'ruhn", "Guild Guide to Vivec", "Guild Guide to Caldera", "Guild Guide to Sadrith Mora"], "notes": "Major trade and quest hub accessible early in gameplay. Starting point for the main quest after arriving from Seyda Neen. Offers essential services, guild opportunities, and substantial quest content."}
Vivec: {"description": "The largest city on Vvardenfell, located on the southern coast. Comprises nine artificial islands (cantons), each a self-contained district. Holy ground home to the Living God Vivec, patrolled by militant Ordinators. Each canton has four tiers: Underworks (sewers), Canalworks (tombs/storage), Waistworks (shops/residences), and Plaza (manors/fine merchants).", "services": ["Mages Guild", "Fighters Guild", "Tribunal Temple", "Imperial Cult", "Multiple shops and merchants across cantons", "Alchemists", "Enchanters", "Booksellers", "Clothiers", "Blacksmiths"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Balmora", "Silt Strider to Molag Mar", "Silt Strider to Seyda Neen", "Silt Strider to Suran", "Boat to Ebonheart", "Boat to Hla Oad", "Boat to Molag Mar", "Boat to Tel Branora", "Guild Guide to Ald'ruhn", "Guild Guide to Balmora", "Guild Guide to Caldera", "Guild Guide to Sadrith Mora", "Gondola between cantons"], "notes": "Best-connected city in the game with access to all transport types. Maze-like layout can be disorienting; gondolas provide affordable navigation. Strict enforcement by Ordinators. North Landing serves as the main hub with silt strider port and harbor."}
Ald'ruhn: {"description": "A dusty settlement aligned with House Redoran, located in the Ashlands near Red Mountain. The Manor District is built inside the hollowed shell of an ancient Emperor land-crab known as Skar. Daily ash storms pose a constant threat of blight exposure.", "services": ["Fighters Guild", "Mages Guild", "Thieves Guild (The Rat In The Pot)", "Tribunal Temple", "Redoran Council Hall", "Clothier", "Alchemist", "Enchanter", "Blacksmith", "Bookseller", "Pawnbroker"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Balmora", "Silt Strider to Gnisis", "Silt Strider to Khuul", "Silt Strider to Maar Gan", "Guild Guide to Balmora", "Guild Guide to Vivec", "Guild Guide to Caldera", "Guild Guide to Sadrith Mora"], "notes": "Fighters Guild has a locked room containing nearly complete glass armor. Guard towers hold crates with bonemold armor. Stick to roads and avoid wilderness due to blighted creatures."}
Sadrith Mora: {"description": "District seat for House Telvanni, meaning 'Forest of the Mushrooms' in Dark Elvish. An island settlement in Zafirbel Bay featuring distinctive architecture of great, magically-formed organic mushrooms arranged around the wizard tower Tel Naga. Accessible only by sea travel and teleportation.", "services": ["Mages Guild (Wolverine Hall)", "Morag Tong", "Alchemist (Anis Seloth)", "Healers/Apothecaries", "Spellmakers (Llaalam Madalas, Urtiso Faryon)", "Blacksmiths", "Enchanter (Galar Rothan)", "Gateway Inn", "Fara's Hole in the Wall"], "travel_connections": ["Boat to Tel Branora", "Boat to Ebonheart", "Boat to Tel Mora", "Boat to Dagon Fel", "Guild Guide to Ald'ruhn", "Guild Guide to Balmora", "Guild Guide to Caldera", "Guild Guide to Vivec"], "notes": "Visitors technically need 'Hospitality Papers' from Angaredhel to explore freely, though enforcement is lax. No silt strider service. Only reachable by boat or teleportation."}
Caldera: {"description": "An Imperial charter town north of Balmora in the West Gash region, built to support nearby ebony mining operations. Features Imperial and Nordic architectural influences arranged in an inverted L-shape between two guard towers.", "services": ["Mages Guild (Guild Guide hub)", "Shenk's Shovel (inn)", "Blacksmith (Hodlismod)", "Pawnbroker (Irgola)", "Clothier (Falanaamo)", "Trader (Verick Gemain)"], "travel_connections": ["Guild Guide to Ald'ruhn", "Guild Guide to Balmora", "Guild Guide to Sadrith Mora", "Guild Guide to Vivec"], "notes": "No silt strider or boat service. Creeper the Scamp is one of the best merchants for selling expensive items. Unguarded alchemy apparatus available in the Mages Guild. Foyada Mamaea to the east provides access to Ghostgate."}
Seyda Neen: {"description": "A small port village on Vvardenfell's southern Bitter Coast, serving as the 'Gateway to Vvardenfell.' Functions primarily as an entry point for Imperial travelers with a distinctive Grand Pharos lighthouse at the harbor.", "services": ["Arrille's Tradehouse (weapons, armor, spells, potions, scrolls)", "Census and Excise Office", "Silt Strider station"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Balmora", "Silt Strider to Gnisis", "Silt Strider to Suran", "Silt Strider to Vivec"], "notes": "Starting location for the game. Complete 'Report to Caius Cosades' quest before reaching level 4. Nearby smugglers' cave Addamasartus and Samarys Ancestral Tomb (containing the valuable Mentor's Ring). Arrille refuses to trade if player carries Moon Sugar or Skooma."}
Suran: {"description": "A town on Lake Masobi's eastern coast in the Ascadian Isles, controlled by House Hlaalu. A hub for pilgrims and adventurers traveling northeast toward Molag Amur and Mount Kand. Also a northern base for Hlaalu slavers conducting business with local plantations.", "services": ["Pawnbroker (Goldyn Belaram)", "Blacksmith (Garothmuk gro-Muzgub)", "Clothier (Verara Rendo)", "Apothecary (Ibarnadad Assirnarari)", "Suran Tradehouse", "Desele's House of Earthly Delights (tavern/inn)", "Tribunal Temple (priest Elynu Saren)"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Balmora", "Silt Strider to Molag Mar", "Silt Strider to Seyda Neen", "Silt Strider to Vivec"], "notes": "Multiple valuable items available for theft with minimal consequences. Road west to Pelagiad along Lake Masobi. Paths east toward Molag Mar."}
Pelagiad: {"description": "A farming town in the Ascadian Isles near Lake Amaya's southern shore. Founded after Vvardenfell opened for settlement in 3E 414, features Western architecture resembling a High Rock town, built around Fort Pelagiad.", "services": ["Halfway Tavern (lodging)", "Blacksmith (Shadbak gra-Burbug at fort, Uulernil in town)", "General Trader (Mebestian Ence)", "Imperial Cult Altar (Fort Pelagiad)", "Thieves Guild (members only, Ladia Flarugrius)"], "travel_connections": ["Divine Intervention returns to Fort Pelagiad", "Road to Seyda Neen (southwest)", "Road to Balmora (northwest)", "Road to Vivec (east)"], "notes": "No direct fast travel. Convenient waypoint between Seyda Neen, Balmora, and Vivec. Almsivi Intervention splits between Balmora and Vivec depending on position."}
Gnisis: {"description": "The largest settlement in northwestern Vvardenfell, a frontier town with many comforts for city-dwellers on their first trip into the wilds. Built on a hillside plain beneath a massive egg mine, controlled by House Redoran with Imperial Legion presence at Fort Darius.", "services": ["Tribunal Temple (shrines to St. Llothis and St. Rilm)", "Shrine of the Mask (Vivec's Ashmask, disease-curing)", "Fort Darius (Imperial Cult and Tribunal shrines)", "Blacksmith (Hannabi Zabynatus)", "Clothier (Zebba Benamamat)", "Madach Tradehouse (general trader)", "Arvs-Drelen wizard tower"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Ald'ruhn", "Silt Strider to Khuul", "Silt Strider to Maar Gan", "Silt Strider to Seyda Neen"], "notes": "Only place to join the Imperial Legion (via General Darius). Gnisis Eggmine contains accessible Dwemer ruins (Bethamez). Cure Blight Disease spell obtainable through Ashmask shrine quest. Talos Cult secret base in barracks cellar."}
Ebonheart: {"description": "The seat of Imperial authority in Vvardenfell. A fortified castle town in the Ascadian Isles where the Duke governs alongside his Grand Council.", "services": ["Six Fishes Inn", "East Empire Company warehouses", "Blacksmith (Sirollus Saccus)", "Hawkmoth Legion Garrison (Imperial Legion HQ)", "Imperial Chapels (Imperial Cult HQ)", "Enchanter (Sauleius Cullian)", "Argonian Mission"], "travel_connections": ["Boat to Vivec", "Boat to Hla Oad", "Boat to Tel Branora", "Boat to Sadrith Mora"], "notes": "Underground caves contain the artifact Lord's Mail, accessible via underwater entrance. No silt strider service. Roads connect to Pelagiad and Seyda Neen. Twin Lamps anti-slavery organization present."}
Hla Oad: {"description": "A small fishing village at the southern end of the Bitter Coast region, southwest of Balmora. Notably sparse with a few desolate fisher huts. A major stopover for smugglers with an underground Camonna Tong base.", "services": ["Fatleg's Drop Off (tavern/general store, run by Trasteve)", "Blacksmith (Dalam Gavyn)", "Pawnbroker (Perien Aurelie)", "Underground Hideout (Camonna Tong base, lockpicks, training)"], "travel_connections": ["Boat to Ebonheart", "Boat to Gnaar Mok", "Boat to Vivec", "Boat to Molag Mar"], "notes": "Minimal legitimate services. Heavy smuggling presence. Almsivi Intervention goes to Balmora, Divine Intervention to Moonmoth Legion Fort. Multiple house faction quests originate here."}
Maar Gan: {"description": "A walled temple town in the Ashlands north of Ald'ruhn, built by House Redoran using distinctive mud construction and insect shells. The settlement closest to the Ghostfence apart from Ghostgate itself.", "services": ["Tribunal Temple Shrine (priest Salen Ravel, spellmaker, potions)", "Andus Tradehouse (tavern and lodging)", "Blacksmith", "General merchant"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Ald'ruhn", "Silt Strider to Khuul", "Silt Strider to Gnisis"], "notes": "Propylon Index can be found on the shrine offering plate. Bonemold armor in guard tower crates. Divine Intervention goes to Buckmoth Legion Fort, Almsivi Intervention to Ald'ruhn. Ground routes lead to Urshilaku Camp."}
Molag Mar: {"description": "A modest town in the Molag Amur region designed as a waypoint for pilgrims. Resembles a single canton of Vivec set down between regions, with an open-air design unlike the domed cantons of Vivec.", "services": ["Tribunal Temple", "St. Veloth's Hostel (lodging and supplies)", "Blacksmith (Hakar the Candle)", "Trader (Vasesius Viciulus)", "The Pilgrim's Rest (tavern)"], "travel_connections": ["Silt Strider to Vivec", "Silt Strider to Suran", "Boat to Vivec", "Boat to Hla Oad", "Boat to Tel Branora"], "notes": "Serves primarily as a resting point for travelers and pilgrims. Two major pilgrimage sites nearby: Mount Kand and Mount Assarnibibi, accessible via foyada (ancient lava tubes). Almsivi Intervention works here; Divine Intervention leads to Wolverine Hall."}
Tel Mora: {"description": "The northernmost Telvanni-held island, home to Mistress Dratha, one of the oldest Telvanni wizard lords. Features mushroom dwellings surrounding a distinctive tower between two islands with connecting walkways, in the Azura's Coast region.", "services": ["Trader (Berwen)", "Clothier (Elegnan)", "Blacksmith (Radras)", "Alchemist (Daynali Dren)", "Apothecary (Jolda)", "The Covenant (tavern)", "Spell Vendors (Diren Vendu, Salama Andrethi, Tinaso Alan)"], "travel_connections": ["Boat to Dagon Fel", "Boat to Sadrith Mora", "Boat to Tel Aruhn", "Boat to Vos"], "notes": "The entire settlement operates under Dratha's female-only preferences. Male characters may shop but should avoid the tower itself. Female players receive warmer welcome and tower access. No silt strider or guild guide service."}
